rationale.R1.1: A general request to review a paper section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution framing and thesis are appropriate.
rationale.R1.2: Reviewing the section centrally involves checking whether the argument proceeds coherently from motivation to method, results, and conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports empirical comparisons, so review centrally includes checking whether the claims follow from the stated results.
rationale.R1.6: A section-level review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is broad and the artifact provides limited experimental context.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about replacing default evaluation protocols, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibilities and not perform the requested review.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for possible downstream harms from endorsing weak or misleading evaluation claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact’s stated claims.
